title: Michaela's Kitchen Island
summary: A 4x4-post kitchen island with a slatted 1x3 shelf, two drawers, and a 2x4 plank countertop — a bigger, more furniture-forward build than this catalog's other kitchen islands.
description:
This island centers on one detail the other islands in this catalog don't have: notched 4x4 legs that accept the shelf stretchers directly, giving the base real post-and-stretcher joinery instead of a screwed-together box frame. Notch the legs before anything else, and modify the notch positions to fit whatever bins or baskets you plan to store on the lower shelf — this is one step worth deciding on before cutting.

The drawer housing is the plan's most failure-prone assembly and is called out as such directly: **it must be built perfectly square**, or the drawers won't glide on their slides. Build the housing as its own unit — back apron, sides, face frame, then fill the face frame gaps with 1x2 spacers — before it ever touches the legs. Attach the completed housing to the upside-down legs with glue and 3" screws, checking square again, and get a second person to help flip the whole assembly right-side up once the glue sets.

The 2x4 countertop is built directly on top of the assembled base, laid out on a flat surface with a deliberate 1-1/2" center gap sized to clear the drawer housing's 2x8 sides — minimizing the gaps between the other boards is the only real skill this step needs. The drawer boxes are built from cut plywood bottoms (cut them square or the drawer won't fit the housing) with 1x4 sides, fronts, and backs; the drawer faces go on last, positioned with a consistent 1" top/bottom reveal and 1/2" gap between drawers for a clean, evenly spaced look.

tools.note: Notching the legs and cutting the plywood drawer bottoms square.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Measure and cut to fit exactly.
Cut square — the housing will not accept a skewed bottom.

steps.title: Notch and prep the legs
steps.body: Notch the legs to accept the shelf stretchers, modifying notch positions if you need to fit taller bins or baskets on the shelf. Attach a spacer to the inside top of each leg, keeping the top inside corner flush — spacer positions differ by leg, so work from your layout, not by assuming symmetry.

steps.title: Build the drawer housing
steps.body: Fasten the back apron to the drawer housing sides and dividers, then attach the face frame pieces. Measure and fill the gaps between the face frame with 1x2 fillers. This housing must be built perfectly square, or the drawer slides won't glide correctly.

steps.title: Attach the housing to the legs
steps.body: Turn everything upside down on a clean, level surface. Attach the drawer housing to the legs, 1" inset from the front and 1/4" inset on the back, using glue and 3" screws. Check for square, then flip the whole assembly right-side up once the glue sets — get help for this, it's heavy.

steps.title: Add the shelf
steps.body: Fasten the shelf tops to the shelf supports with 2" screws, checking for square. There will be a 1" overhang on the ends.

steps.title: Build and attach the countertop
steps.body: On a flat, level surface, lay out the countertop planks and position the top supports on top, taking care to keep everything square. Minimize gaps between planks and leave a 1-1/2" gap in the center to clear the drawer housing's 2x8 sides. Flip the assembled top onto the island and fasten through the supports into the drawer housing sides and face frame.

steps.title: Build and install the drawers
steps.body: Cut the drawer bottoms from the plywood square, then fasten the sides to the bottoms with 2" screws and glue, followed by the fronts and backs. Check your drawer slides before finalizing drawer box dimensions. Install the slides and fit the drawer boxes into the housing.

steps.title: Attach the drawer faces and finish
steps.body: With the drawers fully inserted, position the drawer faces with a 1" gap top and bottom and a 1/2" gap between drawers. Fasten with a few nails from a brad nailer. Attach handles or knobs. Sand and finish the whole island.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Kitchen island on notched 4x4 posts with two drawers and a slatted shelf
